hook.text: Temperature drops about 6.5°C for every 1,000 meters you climb.

buildup.text: Mountains force air upward, cooling it until moisture falls as rain or snow.

discovery.text: This 'orographic effect' makes one side of a mountain wet and the other a desert.

twist.text: Cherrapunji in India gets 11 meters of rain a year because the Himalayas trap monsoon clouds.

climax.text: Mountain weather patterns control agriculture and water supply for billions of people.

punchline.text: Mountains decide where it rains and where it doesn't.

quiz.question: What is the 'orographic effect'?
options: Mountains force air up, causing rain on one side
